commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A contributor-side patch update and real behavior proof are needed; automation cannot supply the contributor’s real environment proof for this security-sensitive PR.; Needs attention: The patch still has a concrete requester-identity trust-boundary issue in WhatsApp reaction dispatch.; Review finding: [P1] Honor requesterSourceProvider for WhatsApp reactions — extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.ts:66 (sha=b96f0bf389281220cc4b70f3439a10f13ad273d6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads trusted sender identity through Discord/WhatsApp message actions, Gateway/MCP loopback, CLI/agent run context, and tests so privileged Discord moderation/admin actions require trusted requester context.

PR surface: Source +483, Tests +730. Total +1213 across 43 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of the PR head shows a concrete path: a WhatsApp `react` with `currentChannelProvider: "whatsapp"`, a different `requesterSourceProvider`, and `requesterSenderId` makes the helper return true and lets the outbound runner use admin-scoped gateway dispatch.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A contributor-side patch update and real behavior proof are needed; automation cannot supply the contributor’s real environment proof for this security-sensitive PR.

target.title: fix(discord): require sender context for moderation actions
target.body:
## Summary

- Require trusted Discord requester context before privileged Discord moderation and guild-admin message actions can use requester identity.
- Carry requester identity through CLI, MCP loopback, and gateway message-action paths without relying on client-controlled sender headers.
- Keep deliverable channel routing on the routeable provider while carrying non-deliverable requester source providers separately.
- Bind sender-scoped MCP loopback tokens to the current request context so requester identity cannot be replayed into another session/channel envelope.

## Verification

- `git diff --name-only origin/main..HEAD -- '*.ts' '*.mjs' | xargs -r ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8`
- `node scripts/check-tsgo-core-boundary.mjs`
- `node scripts/check-no-raw-channel-fetch.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:architecture`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts extensions/discord/src/voice/manager.e2e.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-react-action.test.ts src/channels/plugins/message-actions.security.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.post-connect-health.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/agents/workspace-templates.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts src/crestodian/rescue-message.test.ts src/tasks/task-flow-registry.store.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Discord privileged moderation and guild-admin message actions now require a trusted Discord requester or trusted local owner context, and Gateway/MCP paths no longer let non-admin callers spoof or replay requester sender identity across channel/session boundaries.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux/UTC, Node `v22.22.2`, pnpm `11.2.2`, branch `discord-action-sender-guard`, exact PR head `b96f0bf389281220cc4b70f3439a10f13ad273d6` rebased on `origin/main` `e0018382eb00b8f2bad6ada8ee5f66956ac9cc21`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
git diff --name-only origin/main..HEAD -- '*.ts' '*.mjs' | xargs -r ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/check-tsgo-core-boundary.mjs
node scripts/check-no-raw-channel-fetch.mjs
git diff --check
pnpm check:architecture
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts extensions/discord/src/voice/manager.e2e.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-react-action.test.ts src/channels/plugins/message-actions.security.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.post-connect-health.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/agents/workspace-templates.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts src/crestodian/rescue-message.test.ts src/tasks/task-flow-registry.store.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
```

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the local run on the exact PR head:
```text
$ node --version
v22.22.2
$ pnpm --version

$ git diff --name-only origin/main..HEAD -- '*.ts' '*.mjs' | xargs -r ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1
Checking formatting...

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
(exit 0; no diagnostics)
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
(exit 0; no diagnostics)

$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts extensions/discord/src/voice/manager.e2e.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-react-action.test.ts src/channels/plugins/message-actions.security.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.post-connect-health.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/agents/workspace-templates.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts src/crestodian/rescue-message.test.ts src/tasks/task-flow-registry.store.test.ts
Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 10 passed (10) [unit-fast-fake-timers shard]

[test] passed 10 Vitest shards in 211.32s

$ .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
autoreview target: branch
branch: discord-action-sender-guard
engine: codex
ref: origin/main
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.74)
```

Observed result after fix: Gateway/server tests show non-admin `message.action` calls strip `requesterSenderId` and `senderIsOwner`; outbound runner tests show normal gateway actions stay least-privilege while plugin-declared trusted requester actions request `operator.admin` plus `operator.write`; Discord action tests show prompt/tool-driven privileged moderation rejects non-Discord or missing requester context while trusted Discord requester and local owner paths still work. MCP loopback tests also reject sender-scoped token replay across both different providers and same-provider session/channel/message context changes, and WhatsApp reaction tests preserve trusted requester identity only for same-source WhatsApp reactions.

What was not tested: No live Discord guild moderation command was executed against a real Discord bot/guild. This proof uses the local source checkout, gateway/MCP/Discord/WhatsApp contract tests, lint/type/boundary/architecture gates, and a fresh autoreview on the exact PR head.
target.author: mmaps
